text:
Meteor City (, Ryūseigai) is a junkyard city inhabited by outcasts. People who live there do not exist in any official records, and the existence of the city itself is known to very few people. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 102 

Overview

Not much is known about the city, as much of its information is shrouded in mystery. It is rumored to be at least 1500 years old, and to have been founded as an internment camp set up by a dictator. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 105 Since then, it has been a dumping ground for other countries. The city is said to be the most diverse place on the planet, examples of this include the acceptance of Bonolenov's Gyudondond Tribe and churches with different religious symbols around the city. The population is estimated at eight to ten million people, who live in an area about the size of the Lapet Republic (approximately 5,985.74 km², or 2,311.11 sq. mi.). 

Since most of its inhabitants don't have any personal records, the city has become a huge source of untraceable hitmen and assassins. Taking advantage of this, the Mafia Community invested in the city so they could always have a large reserve of recruits to use at their disposal. However, the Phantom Troupe destroyed this relationship. The inhabitants share remarkable bonds that have been described as "thinner than water, and yet thicker than blood." They have been described as willing to lay down their own lives for their compatriots. This was speculated to be the result of indoctrination from an early age. 

The city is run as a gerontocracy, in which an assembly of elders oversees the city. They are said to make counter-productive decisions over logic. A noteworthy example is when the Chimera Ant Zazan laid siege against the city and started to create her own army of mutant hybrid Ants. Before the assembly decided to retaliate, they argued whether those who had been transformed should be considered dead or alive. This is because many of the victims didn't survive the transformation process and were discarded, while some of those who did survive joined Zazan's army. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 224 In the aftermath of the situation, the assembly argues whether they should expand their retaliation to the real Chimera Ant Queen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 229 It appears to have adequate educational facilities, as several of the city’s children were literate enough to read voice-acting scripts. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 395 Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 396 

History

Mass Abductions

Prior to the establishment of its alliance with the Mafia, Meteor City was the target of intense human trafficking, as its residents didn’t have their existence or humanity recognized by broader society, some thought that it would not be considered illegal to hunt them. There were hundreds of victims annually, with 70% being children under age 15. By the time of Sarasa’s abduction and murder about a decade and a half before the beginning of the series, the traffickers noted that their operations in Meteor City might come to an end soon as the residents were becoming more vigilant. It was this specific incident that was the immediate catalyst for the founding of the Phantom Troupe. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 397 

Outsider Threat

Chrollo tells Hisoka that when someone in the outside world attacked one of Meteor City's residents, the elder used his The Sun and Moon ability to turn one of their compatriots into a bomb in order to deliver a message. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 352 

The Bum Incident

Ten years prior the mayhem caused by the Phantom Troupe in Yorknew City, a bum with no ID was arrested for murder. The authorities didn't find a trace of him ever existing. The bum confesses that he was from Meteor City. He claimed his innocence, but the police prosecuted him anyway and the court found him guilty without giving him an adequate defense. Three years later, the real murderer, a drug addict was arrested and his past crimes were uncovered. It turned out that the bum was innocent after all. 

Right afterward, a total of 31 people consisting of the beat cops, the judge, the prosecutor, his defense and even the jury and witnesses that were involved in the case were assassinated through suicide bombings. The message found near what was left of their bodies read: "We'll accept anything you leave here, but don't ever take anything away from us." Their assassins were 31 Meteor City residents with bombs in their pockets, as it was believed. Each of them approached their intended target, offered them a handshake while smiling, and then blew themselves up, at different locations at exactly the same time. The reason is due to the fact that one of their own was unfairly condemned, so 31 of them gave up their lives to take down another 31 in the name of revenge. 

Kurta Clan Massacre

Five years prior to the 287th Hunter Exam, the Phantom Troupe approached the Kurta Clan for their valuable Scarlet Eyes and massacred all 128 villagers. News of this was reported to the whole world six weeks after the last of them departed. The papers at the time reported that family members were made to face one another and stabbed multiple times, with their heads severed while they were still alive. Pure clan members had their eyes gouged out, while those who married into the clan had their eyes squashed and sustained more brutal wounds than the others. The adult males had the most wounds, but it's assumed that these were sustained during a struggle before they were captured. Those who were not related by blood to the clan were said to be mutilated in order to set an example. After this, the heads of those with Scarlet Eyes were all severed successively. Since the scarlet color is caused by anger and makes the eyes more valuable, it's reported that the Troupe members mutilated the children in front of their parents in order to intensify the scarlet color. Lastly, a message was left behind that read: "We'll accept anything you leave here, but don't ever take anything away from us." Hunter × Hunter - Volume 0, Chapter 2 

Plot

Yorknew City arc

When the Mafia Community attempts to identify the "dead" Phantom Troupe members, a hacker hypothesizes that they might be from Meteor City. He goes on to explain the details of Meteor City and why the identities of the Troupe members will never be discovered. He points out that they are against the wrong people and says that the Ten Dons would agree with him. In a flashback, four original Troupe members, including Chrollo, are seen tossing around a videotape amid the city's garbage heaps. 

After the bounties on the Troupe members are canceled, Melody relays this information to Kurapika. Leorio explains to a confused Gon the history of Meteor City and their way of life. 

Pakunoda has a flashback and remembers the founding of the Troupe at Meteor City, where Chrollo stressed the importance of the Spider as a whole. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 114 

Chimera Ant arc

Half of the members of the Phantom Troupe return to Meteor City to kill Zazan, who has been turning the residents into Ants to use as soldiers. They are successful in defeating the invading Ants and eventually kill their compatriots who have been turned into creatures. After Meruem's death, Bizeff drives an RV with Welfin and Hina inside, saying that he has nowhere to go except Meteor City now that he's banished. Welfin guesses that Gyro is also making his way there and collecting the necessary resources to build his own country. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 30, Chapter 315 

Inhabitants

The Phantom Troupe

The Phantom Troupe was founded in Meteor City, where most of the current members hail from. Leorio pointed out that the Troupe destroyed the ideal partnership between the city and the Mafia, adding that the group may be considered special even within the city. When dangerous intruders invade Meteor City, most of the Spiders volunteer to help, such as when Zazan's Squad invaded the city. 

All of the nine founding members, as well as Shizuku and Kortopi, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 128 were either born on Meteor City or abandoned as babies before having their birth acknowledged. Other Spiders such as Bonolenov and Kalluto also demonstrate loyalty to the city, although they aren't native inhabitants. 

Kikyo Zoldyck

According to the official databook, Kikyo Zoldyck is from Meteor City. This fact was never mentioned in the manga or anime adaptations.

Canary and Gotoh

A character guidebook for the 1999 anime adaptation revealed that both Canary and Gotoh originated from Meteor City. 

In the 2011 anime adaptation, Canary also tells Killua that she will happily act as his guide if he ever wishes to visit someday. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 24 (2011) 

Bizeff, Hina and Welfin

Hina, Welfin, and Bizeff form a trio to travel towards Meteor City to search for Gyro. Bizeff's reason for accompanying the Chimera Ants is that he's now a Class-A political criminal. 

Trivia

In real life, Meteor City was a historic trading post located on Route 66 west of the town of Unincorporated Winslow in Coconino County, Arizona.
The Mafia hacker mentions the city's foundation 1500 years ago. If adjusted for real life timeline, it would have occurred during the Early Middle Ages.
